title: Society for Human Rights founded in Chicago
description: Henry Gerber established the Society for Human Rights in Chicago, regarded as the earliest documented gay rights organization in the United States. It was short-lived, collapsing under legal and social pressure.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: The Advocate begins publication
description: The Advocate started publishing in the United States and grew into a long-running LGBTQ news and culture publication, covering events from the Stonewall uprising through the AIDS crisis and marriage equality.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Stonewall uprising in New York City
description: Patrons of the Stonewall Inn in Greenwich Village resisted a police raid, sparking days of protest. The uprising is widely credited with catalyzing the modern LGBTQ rights movement in the United States.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Jeanne Manford founds PFLAG
description: Jeanne Manford started PFLAG (Parents, Families, and Friends of Lesbians and Gays) in the United States, building a support and advocacy network that expanded to hundreds of local chapters.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Matthew Shepard and James Byrd Jr. Hate Crimes Prevention Act
description: The United States enacted the Matthew Shepard and James Byrd Jr. Hate Crimes Prevention Act, extending federal hate crime protections to cover attacks motivated by sexual orientation, gender identity, and disability.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Repeal of Don't Ask, Don't Tell
description: The United States repealed the Don't Ask, Don't Tell military policy, allowing gay, lesbian, and bisexual service members to serve openly in the armed forces.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Obergefell v. Hodges legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The United States Supreme Court ruled in Obergefell v. Hodges that same-sex couples have a constitutional right to marry, legalizing same-sex marriage nationwide.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Executive Order 13672 bars contractor discrimination
description: Executive Order 13672 prohibited United States federal contractors from discriminating on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity. The Grindr timeline dates this to 2016, but the order was signed in 2014.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Bostock v. Clayton County bars workplace discrimination
description: The United States Supreme Court held in Bostock v. Clayton County that Title VII's ban on sex discrimination protects employees from being fired for being gay or transgender.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Wave of anti-LGBTQ state bills in 2021
description: According to figures cited by the Human Rights Campaign, more than 175 anti-LGBTQ bills were introduced across United States state legislatures in 2021, with over 25 enacted into law.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: 238 anti-LGBTQ bills before state legislatures in 2023
description: The Grindr timeline cites Human Rights Campaign figures reporting 238 anti-LGBTQ bills under consideration in United States state legislatures in 2023, part of a coordinated legislative push against LGBTQ rights.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline
